source_intake_record.raw_question_text:

<image1>

choice_map.D: $$ all of $P, Q, R, S$ and $T$

image_dependency: question asks about specific knots P, Q, R, S, T, which are not described in text and must be identified from the image
image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving likely requires inspecting multiple knot diagrams and determining for each whether it has more than one loop of rope
the task then requires aggregating those findings into one of 5 answer choices
question involves visual classification across several labeled items rather than a single direct text lookup
verifiability: multiple-choice options are provided, so the final answer is constrained and easy to check
answer_text is explicit: 'P, R and T'
the underlying criterion ('more than one loop of rope') is concrete and can be checked against the image

risk_flags: answer_type_inconsistency: top-level answer_type is 'set' while asset_registry_record.answer_manifest.answer_type is 'choice'

decision_reasons: core assets are present and usable: question, answer, and the required image are all available
registry passed with no recorded asset issues, so the sample has sufficient semantic support
initial scoring indicates high image dependency and high verifiability, which makes the item valuable and checkable
the only noted risk is an answer_type metadata inconsistency, but it does not undermine the recoverability of the problem or answer
